decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.27°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective immediate action is to increase the thermal conductivity of the heat spreader to enhance lateral heat dissipation away from the chip hot spot.

changes.reason: Doubling the spreader conductivity (within the allowed 2.0x step ratio) significantly improves lateral heat spreading, reducing the local temperature gradient under the chip.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions.
High conductivity materials may be cost-prohibitive in real-world applications, though this is a simulation study.
